# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio mesh configuration (meshConfig) that allows outbound traffic to any external service. Set outboundTrafficPolicy mode to ALLOW_ANY so services can call external APIs without ServiceEntry definitions. This provides maximum flexibility for microservices that integrate with various external APIs.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: default-outbound-rule
spec:
  host: "*"
---  
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  namespace: ${NAMESPACE} # Required for Namespaces in Istio v2 or later versions, default is the current-context's nameSpace if not defined otherwise  
  name: external-api-gateway      
spec:    
  selector:        
    istio: outboundtrafficpolicy_alloallowany             # This will only match traffic from Istio. You can use it to select the Gateways by other labels too if needed, but this example uses "istio".  		         	      			             	   	       	 				  gw        
     tags: external-service           
---   
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3           # This is used for v2 or later versions of Istio     
kind: VirtualService                 
metadata:                     
   namespace: ${NAMESPACE}            
   name: default                      
spec:                    	    	   		   	 			           					               				  gw             
selectors:              	       							          gateway: external-api-gateway                 service: "*"                                                              outboundTrafficPolicy: ALLOW_ANY                      hosts: [ "%s" ]   #Replace %S with the destination's IP or DNS name. This is required when using Mixed TCP/UDP mode, and should only be used if you have defined at least one VirtualService within a ServiceEntry (outboundTrafficPolicy ALLOW_ANY will implicitly set this.)
